text:
Market today: Tech is getting hit again - AI names specifically are under pressure, and your book is ~90% in exactly that crosshairs. This isn't a random down day; it's a sentiment rotation away from high-multiple tech. Margin means your real drawdown pain is worse than the percentages show.

Your holdings: PLTR is the sharpest concern - already down 17.56% and the sector headwind is aimed directly at it today. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18%, and a TRIM signal means you're carrying too much size into a deteriorating tape. SPAL is down 16.49% with a 2x leveraged structure that bleeds on volatile days like this - it's close to its $19 stop and decays by design. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history; margin amplifies any gap-down risk there.

Watch: (1) Whether AI/chip names stabilize or accelerate lower into Friday's high-volume event - that day could force big moves. (2) SPAL's $19 stop - leveraged ETFs near stops on bad days deserve a hard look before close.

Bottom line: Your most urgent job today is sizing down the losers before margin does it for you.
